import org.slf4j.Logger;

// Same-branch doctrine: a capture and a rethrow pair only when one execution
// path runs both. A terminal guard's throw and the fall-through report are
// exclusive legs - each picks one strategy, so there is no double.
class Jv006GuardShapes {
    private final Logger log;
    private final boolean recreate;

    Jv006GuardShapes(Logger log, boolean recreate) {
        this.log = log;
        this.recreate = recreate;
    }

    // Guard rethrows, fall-through reports and recovers: exclusive, clean.
    void guardThrowThenReport() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) {
            if (!recreate) {
                throw new IllegalStateException("update rejected: " + e.getMessage(), e);
            }
            log.error("recreating after: " + e.getMessage(), e);
            recover();
        }
    }

    // Exclusive if/else legs: one reports, one propagates. Clean.
    void exclusiveLegs() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) {
            if (recreate) {
                log.error("failed", e);
            } else {
                throw new RuntimeException(e);
            }
        }
    }

    // The capture precedes the guard, so the guarded path reports AND
    // rethrows - a real double.
    void reportThenGuardThrow() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) { // want "JV006.*reported at line 47, rethrown at line 49"
            log.error("failed", e);
            if (recreate) {
                throw new RuntimeException(e);
            }
            recover();
        }
    }

    // Both strategies inside the same guarded leg: still a double.
    void doubleInsideGuard() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) { // want "JV006.*reported at line 61, rethrown at line 62"
            if (recreate) {
                log.error("failed", e);
                throw new RuntimeException(e);
            }
            log.error("recovering", e);
            recover();
        }
    }

    // Every path captures at the if join, then the throw doubles them all.
    void captureThenLaterThrow() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) { // want "JV006.*reported at line 75, rethrown at line 79"
            if (recreate) {
                log.error("failed", e);
            } else {
                log.warn("failed", e);
            }
            throw new RuntimeException(e);
        }
    }

    void recover() {}

    void risky() throws Exception {}
}
